evals.prompt: We've been logging AI session retros for a few weeks now (docs/ai-retros/entries/ in this repo). Can you synthesize them into a one-page brief we can bring into our team retrospective on Friday?
evals.expected_output: Produces docs/ai-retros/briefs/brief-<today>.md with: recurring-friction themes ranked by frequency x cost (ambiguous-instruction should rank #1 at 4 of 6 sessions), root-cause distribution grouped into briefing/documentation/codebase/tooling/agent with briefing dominating, adoption tracking (time-window fix adopted by 2026-07-09; live-search verification not verifiable from the log), top 3 evidence-tied actions citing dated entries, and a trend paragraph honest about the 6-entry sample. Stamped ai-retro-brief v1.2.

evals.prompt: Synthesize our AI retro log (docs/ai-retros/entries/) into a brief for tomorrow's retrospective.
evals.expected_output: With only 2 entries present, states that fewer than 3 entries exist, gives a light summary only, and makes NO pattern or trend claims. Does not fabricate recurrence.
